title: Philotheca glabra
intro: Philotheca glabra, also known as the smooth-barked philotheca, is a native Australian plant species belonging to the Rutaceae family. The leaves and bark of Philotheca glabra have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Philotheca glabra include limonoids, flavonoids, and coumarins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Philotheca glabra include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Philotheca glabra is classified within the family Rutaceae, which includes citrus and related plants. The classification of Philotheca glabra is further categorized into the order Sapindales and the kingdom Plantae.
